checks.a_analysis: The output document's text accurately reflects the content of the source PDF. No paragraphs or sentences were fabricated; all body text and headings have a direct basis in the source document. AI-generated elements like the title and table headers are permissible additions for accessibility.
There is no contact information (phone numbers, emails, URLs) present in the source document, and none has been added to the output.
All sections of the single-page resolution, including the headers, title, 'WHEREAS' clauses, 'RESOLVED' clause, adoption date, and signature lines, are present in the output.

The signature lines in the source are represented as a table in the output. The table includes rows for both signees (Taylor McCurry and Steve Pitman), accurately reflecting the data rows present in the source.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: No significant portions of text within any section are omitted. Each clause is captured in its entirety.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting

checks.b_evidence_source: WHEREAS, in September of 2021, US Reps Norma T. Torres and Brian Fitzpatrick announced...
checks.b_evidence_output: WHEREAS, in September of 2021, US Norma T. Torres and Brian Fitzpatrick announced...

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the text content of the output against the source PDF. The paragraphs, including the 'WHEREAS' clauses and the resolution text, appear exactly once in the output, matching the source. While the pipeline includes several image fragments at the end of the document (elements 14-29), these are marked as decorative and do not represent duplication of text content in a way that interferes with document structure.

The output uses an H2 for the document title and an H3 for the 'NOW THEREFORE' resolution section. This represents a logical, nested hierarchy. Although an H1 is omitted, the relative levels are consistent and facilitate navigation.

The signature block at the bottom of the page, which is arranged in two columns in the source, has been converted into a 2-column table. The two signers are placed in separate rows in the first column. This is a common and acceptable linearization of side-by-side content. The generated headers ('Field', 'Value') provide necessary structure for accessibility.

The reading order follows the logical flow of the resolution: header, title, preamble clauses, the resolution itself, and finally the signatures. The Mitchell County seal (image element 13) is moved to follow the text, which is an acceptable reordering for accessibility purposes to prioritize semantic text content over graphical elements.
The source document does not contain any formal bulleted or numbered lists. The series of 'WHEREAS' clauses are correctly identified as individual paragraphs.
There are no hyperlinks present in the source document, and none have been erroneously added to the output.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the HTML output is significantly different from the source PDF. In the original document, the county seal is positioned at the top center as a header element. In the HTML output, it has been moved to the middle of the document, following the main resolution text. Furthermore, the signature block at the bottom has been dismantled; the names are placed into an empty metadata table, while the handwritten signatures are rendered as fragmented images further down the page. The bottom half of the rendered HTML also includes a high-contrast 'block' version of the document that completely rearranges the spatial relationship of the text and images (e.g., 'NORTH CAROLINA' and 'MITCHELL COUNTY' are moved and styled as black headers). This represents a significant deviation from the source's visual organization.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF shows a unified vertical flow: Seal/Header -> Title -> Resolution Text -> Signatures at the bottom.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows the seal moved to the middle, signatures separated into an empty table and fragmented images, and the entire content rearranged into a high-contrast block layout at the bottom.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output includes a 'Signatures' table that is not present in the source PDF. While the 'Value' column in this table is empty, the table structure itself is visually sound, with clear borders and legible headers. It is not 'broken' in terms of rendering (no overlapping text or missing cell boundaries).

Meaningful images from the source, specifically the handwritten signatures, are rendered as broken or fragmented elements. The signature for Taylor McCurry is truncated, missing the leading 'T' and appearing as 'aylor McCurry'. Furthermore, in the high-contrast section at the bottom, the signatures and header graphics are fragmented into individual boxes and fragments (e.g., a standalone 'T' and 'M'), which fails to preserve the meaningful visual integrity of the original signatures.
checks.b_evidence_source: Handwritten signatures for 'Taylor McCurry' and 'Steve Pitman' on the bottom of page 1.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows a signature image truncated to 'aylor McCurry' and additional fragmented signature components in the bottom section.
checks.b_evidence_location: Signature area below the resolution text and within the high-contrast section at the bottom.

checks.a_analysis: The entire body content of the resolution is visually duplicated within the rendered HTML. The resolution text, from the title through to the adoption date, appears once in a standard layout at the top of the page and is repeated in its entirety within the high-contrast block section at the bottom of the page. This is a direct duplication of genuine body content.
checks.b_evidence_source: The text 'RESOLUTION SUPPORTING THE RECLASSIFICATION OF MITCHELL COUNTY 911 DISPATCHERS... Adopted this the 21st day of February, 2023.' appears exactly once.
checks.b_evidence_output: The resolution text starting with 'RESOLUTION SUPPORTING...' appears at the top of the output and is repeated starting near the middle of the screenshot on a black background.
checks.b_evidence_location: The entire main body content of the document.
